name: GastroGuard
tagline: GI symptom tracking and personalized insight engine
summary: Tracks GI symptoms, meals, remedies, and environmental triggers. Uses a structured insight engine with daily rollups, trigger scoring, and longitudinal pattern detection to identify cause-effect relationships.
useCase: For gastritis, reflux, and chronic GI patients trying to identify dietary and behavioral triggers.

name: MindMap+
tagline: Mental state tracking with behavioral pattern recognition
summary: Tracks mood, anxiety, cognitive load, sleep, and behavioral inputs. Converts subjective check-ins into structured data and detects patterns over time.
useCase: For ADHD, anxiety, and performance tracking across time.

name: SkinTrack+
tagline: Lesion and skin condition tracking with environmental correlation
summary: Tracks lesions, breakouts, and skin conditions while correlating them with environmental and behavioral variables like diet, sun exposure, and stress.
useCase: For chronic skin condition patients and longitudinal tracking.

name: GlucoLoop
summary: Builds a tighter feedback loop on top of CGM data (Libre, Dexcom) to generate faster and more actionable glucose insights than standard apps.
useCase: For metabolic optimization and diabetes management.

name: HealthHelper
tagline: Unified health data layer and cross-app analytics engine
summary: Central system designed to unify data across all HeartWire apps and generate cross-domain insights (sleep vs GI vs stress vs behavior).
useCase: Acts as the backend intelligence layer for the entire ecosystem.

name: Modular Knee Brace
tagline: Adaptive biomechanical support system for knee stability and recovery
summary: A modular, adjustable knee brace system designed to improve joint stability, reduce pain, and support rehabilitation. Focused on adaptability, load distribution, and real-world usability.
useCase: For athletes, injury recovery, and long-term joint health.

detail.problem: Knee instability and pain often come from support that is either too generic or too rigid. Off-the-shelf braces rarely adapt to changing swelling, activity level, or limb geometry, so users trade comfort for stability—or the opposite.
detail.design: The system is built from modular components and an adjustable structure so fit and support can be tuned without replacing the entire device. The goal is repeatable alignment and load sharing across sessions, not a single fixed geometry.
engineering: Force distribution: spread joint-adjacent loads across larger surface areas to reduce pressure hot spots and material fatigue.
Joint alignment: maintain consistent hinge axis and varus/valgus control relative to the femoral-tibial line during flexion.
Comfort vs rigidity: calibrate strapping tension and segment stiffness so the brace stays worn during real activity—under-support fails clinically; over-support fails adherence.

name: JonnyJr
summary: Automates research workflows, synthesizes information, and generates structured outputs across projects.
useCase: Personal second brain for engineering and research.
